title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, financial losses, and reputational damage. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread propagation and supply chain attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately audit and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strong authentication, access controls, and regular code reviews.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and monitor cloud environments for unusual activity or unauthorized access.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are stored offline and test recovery processes to minimize downtime in case of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The Oxygen Theme theme for WordPress is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery in all versions...
description: The Oxygen Theme theme for WordPress is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery in all versions up to, and including, 6.0.8 via the laborator_calc_route AJAX action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to make web requests to arbitrary locations originating from the web application and can be used to query and modify information from internal services.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-12886.

title: The Restaurant Cafeteria WordPress theme through 0.4.6 exposes insecure admin-ajax actions...
description: The Restaurant Cafeteria WordPress theme through 0.4.6 exposes insecure admin-ajax actions without nonce or capability checks, allowing any logged-in user, like subscriber, to perform privileged operations. An attacker can install and activate a  from a user-supplied URL, leading to arbitrary PHP code execution, and also import demo content that rewrites site configuration, including Restaurant Cafeteria WordPress theme through 0.4.6_mods, pages, menus, and front page settings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2025-15445.

title: The SureForms – Contact Form, Payment Form & Other Custom Form Builder plugin for WordPress is...
description: The SureForms – Contact Form, Payment Form & Other Custom Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Payment Amount Bypass in all versions up to, and including, 2.5.2. This is due to the create_payment_intent() function performing a payment validation solely based on the value of a user-controlled parameter. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to bypass configured form payment-amount validation and create underpriced payment/subscription intents by setting form_id to 0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass payment validation, potentially leading to financial losses, fraudulent transactions, and compromised subscription systems. This could severely impact businesses relying on the plugin for payment processing.
affected_industries: Small and medium-sized businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Immediately update the SureForms plugin to the latest patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily.
Implement server-side payment validation independent of user-controlled parameters to mitigate exploitation risks.
Monitor payment transactions for suspicious activity and consider deploying a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests.

title: A flaw has been found in wandb OpenUI up to 1.0. This affects the function create_share/get_share...
description: A flaw has been found in wandb OpenUI up to 1.0. This affects the function create_share/get_share of the file backend/openui/server.py of the component HTMLAnnotator Component. Executing a manipulation of the argument ID can lead to HTML injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4992.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS 2.7.6. This issue affects the function...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS 2.7.6. This issue affects the function smf_gx_cca_cb/smf_gy_cca_cb/smf_s6b of the component CCA Message Handler. The manipulation results in denial of service. The attack may be launched remotely. Attacks of this nature are highly complex. The exploitability is assessed as difficult. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS 2.7.6 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service by exploiting a flaw in the CCA Message Handler. This could disrupt network operations, particularly in environments relying on Open5GS for 5G core network functions. While the exploitability is difficult, the public release of the exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Open5GS to the latest patched version if available, or apply any vendor-recommended mitigations.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity or signs of exploitation, particularly targeting the CCA Message Handler.
Implement network segmentation and access controls to limit exposure of Open5GS components to untrusted networks.

title: The Ultimate Member plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all...
description: The Ultimate Member plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all versions up to, and including, 2.11.2. This is due to the '{usermeta:password_reset_link}' template tag being processed within post content via the '[um_loggedin]' shortcode, which generates a valid password reset token for the currently logged-in user viewing the page. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to craft a malicious pending post that,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Contributor-level access or higher to expose sensitive information, including valid password reset tokens. This could lead to unauthorized account access, privilege escalation, and data breaches.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Ultimate Member plugin to a patched version once available or disable the plugin temporarily if it is not critical to site functionality.
Restrict Contributor-level access to only trusted users and review user roles and permissions for unnecessary privileges.
Monitor logs for any suspicious activity, such as unauthorized password reset requests or unusual post submissions.

title: A vulnerability was detected in QDOCS Smart School Management System up to 7.2. The impacted...
description: A vulnerability was detected in QDOCS Smart School Management System up to 7.2. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file /admin/enquiry of the component Admission Enquiry Module. Performing a manipulation of the argument Note results in cross site scripting. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4991.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in chatwoot up to 4.11.1. The affected element is an...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in chatwoot up to 4.11.1. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /app/login of the component Signup Endpoint. Such manipulation of the argument signupEnabled with the input true leads to improper authorization. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4990.

title: A vulnerability was identified in dloebl CGIF up to 0.5.2. This vulnerability affects the...
description: A vulnerability was identified in dloebl CGIF up to 0.5.2. This vulnerability affects the function cgif_addframe of the file src/cgif.c of the component GIF Image Handler. The manipulation of the argument width/height leads to integer overflow. The attack may be initiated remotely. The identifier of the patch is b0ba830093f4317a5d1f345715d2fa3cd2dab474. It is suggested to install a patch to address this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the GIF Image Handler could allow remote attackers to exploit integer overflow, potentially leading to application crashes, denial of service, or arbitrary code execution. This poses a risk to systems processing untrusted or malicious GIF files.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch identified by b0ba830093f4317a5d1f345715d2fa3cd2dab474 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit systems and applications using dloebl CGIF up to version 0.5.2 to identify potential exposure.
Implement strict input validation and monitoring for GIF files to detect and block malicious payloads.

title: UniFi Network Controller before version 5.10.22 and 5.11.x before 5.11.18 contains an improper...
description: UniFi Network Controller before version 5.10.22 and 5.11.x before 5.11.18 contains an improper certificate verification vulnerability that allows adjacent network attackers to conduct man-in-the-middle attacks by presenting a false SSL certificate during SMTP connections. Attackers can intercept SMTP traffic and obtain credentials by exploiting the insecure SSL host verification mechanism in the SMTP certificate validation process.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2019-25652.

description: Substance3D - Stager versions 3.1.7 and earlier are affected by a Use After Free vulnerability that could result in arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user. Exploitation of this issue requires user interaction in that a victim must open a malicious file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-27309.

description: Ubiquiti UniFi Network Controller prior to 5.10.12 (excluding 5.6.42), UAP FW prior to 4.0.6, UAP-AC, UAP-AC v2, and UAP-AC Outdoor FW prior to 3.8.17, USW FW prior to 4.0.6, USG FW prior to 4.4.34 uses AES-CBC encryption for device-to-controller communication, which contains cryptographic weaknesses that allow attackers to recover encryption keys from captured traffic. Attackers with adjacent network access can capture sufficient encrypted traffic and exploit AES-CBC mode vulnerabilities to der

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2019-25651.

title: OpenClaw: Telegram Webhook Missing Guess Rate Limiting Enables Brute-Force Guessing of Weak Webhook Secret
description:
## Summary

Telegram Webhook Missing Guess Rate Limiting Enables Brute-Force Guessing of Weak Webhook Secret

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.3.24`
- First patched version: `2026.3.25`
- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

Telegram webhook auth previously rejected bad secrets but did not throttle repeated guesses, allowing brute-force attempts against weak webhook secrets. Commit `c2c136ae9517ddd0789d742

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The lack of rate limiting on Telegram webhook secret validation allows attackers to perform brute-force attacks, potentially compromising webhook secrets and enabling unauthorized access or manipulation of sensitive data.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the `openclaw` package to version `2026.3.25` or later to apply the patch.
Implement strong webhook secrets with high entropy to reduce the risk of brute-force attacks.
Enable additional rate limiting mechanisms at the application level to mitigate brute-force attempts.

BlueBubbles group reaction events previously bypassed `requireMention` and still enqueued agent-visible system events in groups that were supposed to stay mention-gated. Commit `f8c9863

title: OpenClaw: Matrix Verification Notices Bypass Matrix DM Policy and Reply to Unpaired DM Peers
description:
## Summary

Matrix Verification Notices Bypass Matrix DM Policy and Reply to Unpaired DM Peers

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.3.24`
- First patched version: `2026.3.25`
- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

Matrix verification notices previously bypassed DM access checks and could reply to peers that were unpaired or otherwise outside the allowed DM policy. Commit `2383daf5c4a4e08d9553e0e949552ad755ef9

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized communication with unpaired DM peers, potentially leading to data leakage, unauthorized access, or manipulation of sensitive information within Matrix-based applications.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the affected package to version 2026.3.25 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit current Matrix-based systems to identify any unauthorized DM communications or anomalies.
Implement stricter access controls and monitoring for DM policies to prevent unauthorized interactions.

title: OpenClaw: BlueBubbles Webhook Missing Rate Limiting Enables Brute-Force Password Guessing
description:
## Summary

BlueBubbles Webhook Missing Guess Rate Limiting Enables Brute-Force Guessing of Weak Webhook Password

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.3.24`

- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

BlueBubbles webhook auth previously rejected wrong passwords without throttling repeated guesses, allowing brute-force attempts against weak webhook passwords. Commit `5e08ce36d52

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The lack of rate limiting on the BlueBubbles webhook allows attackers to perform brute-force attacks to guess weak passwords, potentially leading to unauthorized access and data compromise.

Implement additional security controls, such as IP-based rate limiting or CAPTCHA, to prevent automated attacks.

title: OpenClaw: Gateway Plugin HTTP Auth Grants Unrestricted operator.admin Runtime Scope to All Callers
description:

Gateway Plugin HTTP auth: "gateway" Mints operator.admin Runtime Scope

## Details

Gateway-authenticated plugin HTTP routes previously created a runtime scope set that included `operator.admin` regardless of caller-granted scopes. Commit `ec2dbcff9afd8a52e00de054b506c91726d9fbbe` keeps plugin

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: OpenClaw: Silent privilege escalation via gateway shared-auth reconnect

- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

Silent local shared-auth reconnects could previously auto-approve `scope-upgrade` requests and widen a paired device from `operator.read` to `operator.ad

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity.

title: OpenClaw: Gateway Backend Reconnect lets Non-Admin Operator Scopes Self-Claim operator.admin
description:
## Summary

Gateway Backend Reconnect lets Non-Admin Operator Scopes Self-Claim operator.admin

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Affected versions: `<= 2026.3.24`
- First patched version: `2026.3.25`
- Latest published npm version at verification time: `2026.3.24`

## Details

Backend-labeled reconnects could previously self-request broader scopes and bypass pairing, allowing non-admin operators to reconnect as `operator.admin`. Commit `d3d8e316bd819d3c7e34253aeb7eccb2510

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows non-admin operators to escalate privileges to 'operator.admin', potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, and system manipulation. This could compromise sensitive data and critical operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade the 'openclaw' package to version 2026.3.25 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit all systems using the affected package to identify any unauthorized privilege escalations and revoke improper access.
Implement stricter monitoring and logging for backend reconnections to detect and prevent scope manipulation.

title: OpenClaw: Gateway HTTP /sessions/:sessionKey/kill Reaches Admin Kill Path Without Caller Scope Binding
description:
## Summary

Gateway HTTP /sessions/:sessionKey/kill Reaches Admin Kill Path Without Caller Scope Binding.

## Details

The HTTP route previously treated any bearer-authenticated request as admin-eligible and could call without binding the action to requester ownership or caller-granted operator scopes. The flaw removes the bearer-token admin fallback and keeps remote session kills on the local-admin or requester-owned path only.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: CVE-2026-3368 - The Injection Guard plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via malicious
description: The Injection Guard plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via malicious query parameter names in all versions up to and including 1.2.9. This is due to insufficient input sanitization in the sanitize_ig_data() function which only sanitizes array values but not array keys, combined with missing output escaping in the ig_settings.php template where stored parameter keys are echoed directly into HTML. When a request is made to the site, the plugin captures the query string via $_SERVER['QUERY_STRING'], applies esc_url_raw() (which preserves URL-encoded special characters like %22, %3E, %3C), then passes it to parse_str() which URL-decodes the string, resulting in decoded HTML/JavaScript in the array keys. These keys are stored via update_option('ig_requests_log') and later rendered without esc_html() or esc_attr() on the admin log page. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in the admin log page that execute whenever an administrator views the Injection Guard log interface.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3368.

title: CVE-2026-3350 - The Image Alt Text Manager plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the
description: The Image Alt Text Manager plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the post title in all versions up to, and including, 1.8.2. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping when dynamically generating image alt and title attributes using a DOM parser. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Author-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3350.

title: CVE-2026-3339 - The Keep Backup Daily plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Limited Path Traversal in all versions u
description: The Keep Backup Daily plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Limited Path Traversal in all versions up to, and including, 2.1.1 via the `kbd_open_upload_dir` AJAX action. This is due to insufficient validation of the `kbd_path` parameter, which is only sanitized with `sanitize_text_field()` - a function that does not strip path traversal sequences. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Administrator-level access and above, to list the contents of arbitrary directories on the server outside of the intended uploads directory.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Administrator-level access to list the contents of arbitrary directories on the server, potentially exposing sensitive information. However, the risk is mitigated by the requirement for high-level access privileges.
recommended_actions: Update the Keep Backup Daily plugin to the latest version once a patch is released.
Restrict Administrator-level access to trusted users only and review user permissions regularly.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity related to directory traversal attempts.

title: CVE-2026-33428 - Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. Prior to versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and
description: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. Prior to versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and 2026.1.2, a non-staff user with elevated group membership could access deleted posts belonging to any user due to an overly broad authorization check on the deleted posts index endpoint. Versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and 2026.1.2 contain a patch. No known workarounds are available.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33428.

title: CVE-2026-33427 - Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. Prior to versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and
description: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. Prior to versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and 2026.1.2, an unauthenticated attacker can cause a legitimate Discourse authorization page to display an attacker-controlled domain, facilitating social engineering attacks against users. Versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and 2026.1.2 contain a patch. No known workarounds are available.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33427.

description: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. title: CVE-2026-33425 - Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. Prior to versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and
description: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. Prior to versions 2026.3.0-latest.1, 2026.2.1, and 2026.1.2, unauthenticated users can determine whether a specific user is a member of a private group by observing changes in directory results when using the `exclude_groups` parameter. description: Discourse is an open-source discussion platform. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33424.

title: CVE-2026-33238 - WWBN AVideo is an open source video platform. Prior to version 26.0, the `listFiles.json.php` endpoi
description: WWBN AVideo is an open source video platform. Prior to version 26.0, the `listFiles.json.php` endpoint accepts a `path` POST parameter and passes it directly to `glob()` without restricting the path to an allowed base directory. An authenticated uploader can traverse the entire server filesystem by supplying arbitrary absolute paths, enumerating `.mp4` filenames and their full absolute filesystem paths wherever they exist on the server — including locations outside the web root, such as private or premium media directories. Version 26.0 contains a patch for the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33238.

title: CVE-2026-33237 - WWBN AVideo is an open source video platform. Prior to version 26.0, the Scheduler plugin's `run()`
description: WWBN AVideo is an open source video platform. Prior to version 26.0, the Scheduler plugin's `run()` function in `plugin/Scheduler/Scheduler.php` calls `url_get_contents()` with an admin-configurable `callbackURL` that is validated only by `isValidURL()` (URL format check). Unlike other AVideo endpoints that were recently patched for SSRF (GHSA-9x67-f2v7-63rw, GHSA-h39h-7cvg-q7j6), the Scheduler's callback URL is never passed through `isSSRFSafeURL()`, which blocks requests to RFC-1918 private addresses, loopback, and cloud metadata endpoints. An admin can configure a scheduled task with an internal network `callbackURL` to perform SSRF against cloud infrastructure metadata services or internal APIs not otherwise reachable from the internet. Version 26.0 contains a patch for the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33237.

title:
CVE-2026-32666 - WebCTRL systems that communicate over BACnet inherit the protocol's lack
 of network layer authentic
description:
WebCTRL systems that communicate over BACnet inherit the protocol's lack
 of network layer authentication. WebCTRL does not implement additional 
validation of BACnet traffic so an attacker with network access could 
spoof BACnet packets directed at either the WebCTRL server or associated
 AutomatedLogic controllers. Spoofed packets may be processed as 
legitimate.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32666.

title: CVE-2026-2430 - The Autoptimize plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the lazy-loadi
description: The Autoptimize plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the lazy-loading image processing in all versions up to, and including, 3.1.14. This is due to the use of an overly permissive regular expression in the `add_lazyload` function that replaces all occurrences of `\ssrc=` in image tags without limiting to the actual attribute. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page by crafting an image tag where the `src` URL contains a space followed by `src=`, causing the regex to break the HTML structure and promote text inside attribute values into executable HTML attributes.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2430.

title: CVE-2026-2352 - The Autoptimize plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'ao_post_p
description: The Autoptimize plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'ao_post_preload' meta value in all versions up to, and including, 3.1.14. This is due to insufficient input sanitization in the `ao_metabox_save()` function and missing output escaping when the value is rendered into a `<link>` tag in `autoptimizeImages.php`. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page, granted the "Image optimization" or "Lazy-load images" setting is enabled in the plugin configuration.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2352.

title:
CVE-2026-25086 - Under certain conditions, an attacker could bind to the same port used 
by WebCTRL. This could allow
description:
Under certain conditions, an attacker could bind to the same port used 
by WebCTRL. This could allow the attacker to craft and send malicious 
packets and impersonate the WebCTRL service without requiring code 
injection into the WebCTRL software.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-25086.

title:
CVE-2026-24060 - Service information is not encrypted when transmitted as BACnet packets 
over the wire, and can be s
description:
Service information is not encrypted when transmitted as BACnet packets 
over the wire, and can be sniffed, intercepted, and modified by an 
attacker. Valuable information such as the File Start Position and File 
Data can be sniffed from network traffic using Wireshark's BACnet 
dissector filter. The proprietary format used by WebCTRL to receive 
updates from the PLC can also be sniffed and reverse engineered.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-24060.
